title: taenitis cordata
intro: Taenitis cordata, also known as the New Zealand fern or kupenga, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Taenitis cordata include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and relieving pain. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to support skin health and immune function.
